task_title: Annotate slide 6 with blue directional arrows
instruction: Add two small up arrows below the ports image and match them to the first arrow's styling.

window_merge_decisions.reason: The next step directly continues configuring the same first gradient stop color: it starts from the palette opened in the previous step and proceeds to the custom color dialog for that same control.

The next step directly continues the same gradient-stop color edit: the palette opened in s20 is the visible starting state in s21, and s21 simply drills into the custom color picker for that same second stop.

task_goal: Two aligned up-arrow annotations appear beneath the ports image, using the same visual style as the first arrow.

action_preconditions: Slide 6 is active and no shape menu is open.

nl_position: the Insert label in the top menu bar

steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx". The current slide displayed is slide 6 of 23, titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide", featuring content about a "Button Style Podium" with images of control panels and connectors. On the left, a vertical slide thumbnail pane shows previews of all slides. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The main editing area displays the slide content with text describing how to connect a laptop using VGA/audio cables, including an image of a cable connector. On the right, the Properties panel is open, showing Slide properties such as Format (On-screen w:4:3), Orientation (Landscape), Background (None), and Master Slide settings. Below that are layout thumbnails. The status bar at the bottom indicates zoom level (118%) and language (English USA). The system clock in the top bar reads Mar 26 10:44. The desktop environment appears to be GNOME on Linux, with application icons visible on the left sidebar.
steps.nl_explanation: Open the Insert menu to add another arrow.

nl_position: the Shape item in the Insert dropdown menu

steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open, displaying slide 6 of 23 titled “Laptop Computer” from a presentation named “multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx”. The Insert menu is open, with the Shape submenu expanded, showing options like Line, Basic Shapes, Symbol Shapes, etc. The Properties panel on the right shows Slide settings (Format: On-screen presentation 4:3, Orientation: Landscape, Background: None) and Layouts with multiple thumbnail options. The slide content includes text about connecting a laptop via VGA/audio cable, images of a control panel and an audio cable, and a sidebar listing topics like “SuperPole Panel”, “Document Camera”, and “Tips and Help Line”. The top menu bar shows File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, etc., with the Insert dropdown active.
steps.nl_explanation: Choose Shape again to add another directional marker.

action_preconditions: The Block Arrows submenu is open.

nl_position: the Up Arrow item in the Block Arrows submenu

steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open with a presentation titled "multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx" displayed. The Insert menu is open, with the Shape submenu expanded, showing various shape options including "Block Arrows" and its sub-options like "Right Arrow", "Left Arrow", "Down Arrow", etc., with "Up Arrow" currently highlighted. The main slide shows a title "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide" and includes images of audio/video ports and cables, along with text about connecting devices. The Properties panel on the right displays Slide settings (Format: On-screen, Orientation: Landscape) and Layouts thumbnails. The left sidebar shows slide thumbnails for slides 1–8. The status bar at the bottom indicates "Slide 6 of 23" and zoom level "118%".
action_after_effects: The menu closes and the cursor enters up-arrow drawing mode.
steps.nl_explanation: Select the Up Arrow shape so a new arrow can be drawn below the port image.

steps.step_goal: Draw the first small up arrow
action_preconditions: The Up Arrow drawing tool is active.
steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open, displaying slide 6 of 23 from a presentation titled “multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx”. The active slide is labeled “Button Style Podium” and features a large title, an image of a podium control panel with a blue downward arrow, a close-up of HDMI/VGA/USB ports with a red crosshair cursor over the VGA port, and a photo of an audio cable. A black sidebar on the left lists slide topics including “On/Off Button Volume Control”, “Lectern Computer”, “Document Camera”, and “Tips and Help Line”. The Properties panel on the right shows Slide settings (Format: On-screen, Landscape orientation, None background) and Layouts thumbnails. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, etc., and the status bar at the bottom indicates zoom level (118%) and language (English USA).
action_after_effects: A small up arrow is drawn below the ports image and stays selected.
steps.nl_explanation: Drag beneath the ports photo to place a small up arrow pointing upward.

steps.step_goal: Move the small arrow up slightly
action_preconditions: The new small up arrow is selected.
steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide." The current slide (Slide 6 of 23) is focused on the "Button Style Podium" section, displaying an image of a control panel with labeled buttons such as “ON/OFF,” “MUTE,” and “VOLUME.” To the right, there are images showing HDMI, VGA, audio, and USB ports, along with a cable connector. The slide includes instructional text about connecting a laptop computer using VGA/audio cables and mentions adapter needs if the laptop lacks compatible outputs. A vertical navigation pane on the left shows thumbnails of all slides. On the right, the Properties panel is open, showing formatting options for selected shapes or text, including font, paragraph spacing, area fill, and line settings. The selected object appears to be a shape or image, as indicated by the “Shape selected” status at the bottom. The system clock in the top bar reads Mar 26 10:44.
action_after_effects: The selected up arrow moves slightly upward.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Up to nudge the small arrow closer to the photo.

steps.step_goal: Move the small arrow slightly left
action_preconditions: The small up arrow is still selected.
steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open, displaying slide 6 of 23 titled “Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide.” The active slide features a large title, a blue downward arrow pointing to an image of a podium control panel, and a close-up photo of an HDMI cable. A sidebar on the left shows thumbnails of all 23 slides. The Properties panel on the right is open to the “Character” and “Area” sections, with the “Area” settings visible (Fill: Color, Transparency: None, Corner style: Rounded). A shape (a blue upward arrow) is selected on the slide, as indicated by the status bar at the bottom: “Slide 6 of 23 — Shape selected.” The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The window title reads “multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx - LibreOffice Impress.”
action_after_effects: The selected up arrow shifts slightly left.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Left to improve the arrow alignment under the ports.

action_preconditions: Both the large down arrow and the small up arrow are visible on the slide.

nl_position: the large down arrow above the left podium image

steps.action_before_state:
The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide." The current slide (Slide 6 of 23) is focused on the "Button Style Podium" section, displaying an image of a control panel with labeled buttons such as “ON/OFF,” “VOLUME,” “MUTE ON/OFF,” and input ports like HDMI, VGA, AUDIO, and USB. There’s also an inset image showing a cable connector.

On the left, a slide thumbnail pane displays previews of all 23 slides, with Slide 6 currently selected. The main editing area shows the slide content with text explaining how to connect a laptop using VGA/audio cables and notes about resolution settings (1280x800 or 720p).

The right sidebar contains the Properties panel, currently showing formatting options for the selected shape — including Character (font: Liberation Sans, size 18 pt), Paragraph spacing, Area fill and transparency, and Line styling. A blue arrow shape is selected on the slide, indicated by green handles around it.

The top menu bar includes standard options: File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The system clock at the top indicates March 26, 10:44. The application window is maximized, and the desktop environment appears to be Linux-based (Ubuntu or similar).
action_after_effects: The large down arrow becomes selected.
steps.nl_explanation: Select the large arrow to use its formatting as the source style.

action_preconditions: The large down arrow is selected.

nl_position: the paintbrush Clone Formatting button on the top toolbar

steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows the LibreOffice Impress application open with a presentation titled "multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx". The current slide, labeled as Slide 6 of 23, is titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide" and focuses on the "Button Style Podium" section. It includes images of a podium control panel with labeled ports (HDMI, VGA, Audio, USB) and a laptop connection guide. On the left, a slide navigation pane displays thumbnails of all slides. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Tools, Window, and Help. The right sidebar shows the Properties panel with tabs for Character, Paragraph, Area, and Line, currently displaying formatting options for selected shapes or text. A blue arrow shape is selected on the slide, indicated by handles around it, and its properties are visible in the sidebar. The system clock at the top indicates March 26 at 10:44. The desktop environment appears to be Linux-based, with application icons visible on the left dock.
action_after_effects: Clone Formatting mode is activated from the toolbar.
steps.nl_explanation: Click Clone Formatting to copy the large arrow's styling to another shape.

steps.step_goal: Apply the copied formatting to the small arrow
action_preconditions: Clone Formatting mode is active and the small up arrow is available as the target.

nl_position: the small up arrow beneath the ports image

steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open with a presentation titled “multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx”, currently displaying slide 6 of 23. The slide is titled “Button Style Podium” and includes images of a podium control panel, HDMI/VGA/AUDIO/USB ports, and a laptop cable, along with descriptive text about connecting a laptop and using the podium’s features. On the left, the Slides pane shows thumbnails of all 23 slides. The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The toolbar below contains standard editing icons. On the right, the Properties panel is open to the “Character” and “Paragraph” sections, with “Area” and “Line” properties visible below; a shape (a red crosshair) is selected on the slide, as indicated by the status bar reading “Slide 6 of 23 — Shape selected.” The system clock shows Mar 26 10:44.
action_after_effects: The small up arrow is clicked, receives the copied formatting, and becomes selected.
steps.nl_explanation: Click the small arrow to apply the copied style from the large arrow.

action_preconditions: The formatted small up arrow is selected.
steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open, displaying slide 6 of 23 titled “Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide.” The slide contains text sections including “Button Style Podium,” “Laptop Computer,” and “Document Camera,” with images of a podium control panel, HDMI/VGA/USB ports, and an audio cable. A blue arrow shape points downward toward the port image, and a red crosshair cursor hovers over that area. The Properties sidebar is open on the right, showing formatting options for the selected shape under “Area” (Gradient fill, Linear, 180°, colors #3E7FCC and #A4C1FF) and “Line” settings. The top menu bar shows File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The status bar indicates “Shape selected” and zoom level 118%.
action_after_effects: Dragging creates a duplicate up arrow to the right of the original.
steps.nl_explanation: Drag the selected small arrow to create a copied arrow for a second highlighted port.

steps.step_goal: Move the duplicate arrow up slightly
action_preconditions: The new duplicate up arrow is selected.
steps.action_before_state: LibreOffice Impress is open, displaying slide 6 of 23 from a presentation titled “multimedia_classroom_podium-2020.pptx”. The active slide is titled “Button Style Podium” and features a large blue downward arrow pointing to an image of a control panel with labeled buttons (ON/OFF, MUTE ON/OFF, PC/LAPTOP/DOC CAM, HDMI), alongside images of HDMI, VGA, AUDIO, and USB ports, and a cable with a 3.5mm jack. A sidebar on the left shows thumbnails of all 23 slides. The Properties panel on the right is open to the “Character” and “Paragraph” sections, with “Area” and “Line” properties visible below. The selected object is a shape (indicated by green handles and a red crosshair), and the status bar at the bottom reads “Slide 6 of 23 — Shape selected.” The top menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, Help. The system clock shows Mar 26 10:45.
action_after_effects: The selected duplicate arrow moves slightly upward.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Up to raise the duplicated arrow closer to the ports image.

steps.step_goal: Move the duplicate arrow slightly left
action_preconditions: The duplicated right-hand arrow remains selected.
steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide." The current slide (Slide 6 of 23) is focused on the "Button Style Podium" section, displaying an image of a control panel with labeled buttons such as “ON/OFF,” “VOLUME,” “MUTE,” and “LAPTOP.” There are also images showing HDMI, VGA, and audio ports, along with a cable connector. Text explains how to connect a laptop using VGA/audio cables and mentions adapter needs if the laptop lacks compatible outputs. A sidebar on the left shows thumbnails of all slides. On the right, the Properties panel is open, showing formatting options for selected shapes or text, including font, paragraph spacing, area fill (gradient), and line settings. A blue arrow shape is currently selected on the slide, indicated by handles around it. The application menu bar at the top includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The system clock in the top bar reads Mar 26 10:45.
action_after_effects: The selected duplicate arrow shifts slightly left.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Left to refine the duplicate arrow's horizontal position.

steps.step_goal: Move the duplicate arrow slightly left again
action_preconditions: The duplicated right-hand arrow is still selected after one left nudge.
steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide." The current slide (Slide 6 of 23) is focused on the "Button Style Podium" section, displaying an image of a control panel with labeled buttons such as “ON/OFF,” “MUTE,” and “VOLUME.” To the right, there are images illustrating HDMI, VGA, and audio ports, along with a cable connector. A blue arrow points to the VGA port, and another points to the HDMI port, both highlighted with green selection handles. On the left, a slide thumbnail pane shows all slides in the presentation. The Properties sidebar on the right is open, showing formatting options for the selected shape — currently set to a gradient fill and rounded corners. The active window is LibreOffice Impress, and the system’s top bar indicates the date and time as March 26, 10:45. The application menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The bottom status bar shows the current slide number, total slides, and zoom level (118%).
action_after_effects: The selected duplicate arrow moves a little further left.
steps.nl_explanation: Press Left again to finish aligning the duplicated arrow.

steps.step_goal: Deselect the arrows and review the layout
action_preconditions: Two small up arrows are visible beneath the ports image and the right one is selected.

nl_position: a blank white area to the right of the images on the slide

steps.action_before_state: The screenshot shows LibreOffice Impress open with a presentation titled "Multimedia Classroom Podium Tutorial and User Guide." The current slide (Slide 6 of 23) is focused on the "Button Style Podium" section, displaying an image of a control panel with labeled buttons such as “ON/OFF,” “MUTE ON,” and “VOLUME.” To the right, there are images showing HDMI, VGA, audio, and USB ports, along with a cable connector. A blue arrow points to the VGA port, and another points to the HDMI port. On the left, a slide navigation pane lists all slides with thumbnails. The Properties panel on the right is open, showing formatting options for selected shapes or text, including font, paragraph spacing, area fill (gradient), and line settings. The selected object appears to be a shape or line, as indicated by the “Shape selected” status at the bottom. The application menu bar includes File, Edit, View, Insert, Format, Slide, Slide Show, Tools, Window, and Help. The system clock in the top bar reads Mar 26 10:45.
action_after_effects: The arrows are deselected, leaving the final annotation layout visible.
steps.nl_explanation: Click empty slide space to finish positioning the arrows and review the result.
